key_insight: Detonation-sprayed titanium dioxide metal-ceramic coatings can have their roughness, thickness, and hydrophobicity tuned by adjusting spraying parameters such as nozzle speed and spray distance.
engineering_relevance: The study provides process-structure-property relationships and predictive equations that can help optimize coating production for higher hydrophobicity in surface engineering applications.
method: Varied detonation spraying process conditions for titanium-based coatings
Measured surface roughness, coating thickness, and contact angle/hydrophobicity
Fitted parameter-dependent trends, including inverse power and parabolic laws
evidence_signal: The abstract reports empirical dependence of roughness, thickness, and contact angle on process parameters, and states that new equations were derived to predict coating characteristics and optimal spray settings.
watch_item: The results appear process- and material-specific, so the equations and optimal settings should be validated before transferring them to other coating chemistries or spray systems.
paper_title: Surface Characteristics of Titanium Based Coatings Obtained by Detonation Spraying Under Various Process Conditions
